message:
🌱 Add keycloak: Support multiple mail domains for linked IDPs per mission (#873)

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-33804-support-multiple-mail-domains-for-linked-idps-per-organization",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Support multiple mail domains for linked IDPs per organization",
+    "description": "Support multiple mail domains for linked IDPs per organization. Requested by 27+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current Keycloak setup",
+        "description": "Verify your Keycloak version supports organizations with multiple mail domains:\n```bash\n# If running in a container:\ndocker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config\n\n# Check the container image version:\ndocker inspect keycloak | grep -i image\n```\nThis feature requires Keycloak 26+ with the Organizations feature enabled."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Understand the multi-domain requirement",
+        "description": "Keycloak's Organizations feature allows linking Identity Providers (IDPs) to organizations based on email domains. Previously, only a single mail domain could be configured per organization, which was limiting for companies that own multiple email domains (e.g., `company.com`, `company.co.uk`, `subsidiary.com`).\n\nWithout multi-domain support, administrators had to create duplicate IDPs or separate organizations for each domain."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Configure multiple mail domains for an organization",
+        "description": "In the Keycloak Admin Console, configure multiple mail domains for an organization:\n1. Go to **Organizations** and select or create an organization\n2. Under the organization's **Domains** section, add multiple email domains\n3. Link an IDP to the organization\n4. Users with email addresses matching any of the configured domains will be routed to the linked IDP\n\nAlternatively, use the Admin REST API:\n```bash\ncurl -X PUT -H \"Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN\" \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"domains\": [{\"name\": \"company.com\"}, {\"name\": \"company.co.uk\"}]}' \\\n  https://your-keycloak/admin/realms/your-realm/organizations/$ORG_ID\n```\n\nSee the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/35671) for implementation details."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify multi-domain routing works",
+        "description": "Test that users from different email domains are correctly routed to the organization's linked IDP:\n1. Try logging in with a user whose email matches the first domain (e.g., `user@company.com`)\n2. Try logging in with a user whose email matches the second domain (e.g., `user@company.co.uk`)\n3. Both should be redirected to the same organization-linked IDP"
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Keycloak added support for configuring multiple mail domains per organization, allowing a single IDP to serve users from multiple email domains within the same organization. This eliminates the need to create duplicate IDPs or separate organizations for each domain.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "community",
+      "identity",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/33804",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/35671"
+    },
+    "reactions": 27,
+    "comments": 27,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "docker"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Keycloak 26+ server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with the Organizations feature enabled."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:49:05.051Z",

+    "sanitized": true,

+}
